name: Ming Shrimp with Hot Sauce B1
recipeInstructions:
1. Marinate the shrimp in salt, pepper, egg white, and cornstarch for 30 minutes.
2. Heat a wok until hot and dry.
3. Add oil to the heated wok.
4. When the oil begins to smoke, add the shrimp and stir quickly so that the pieces separate.
5. After 2 minutes, drain the shrimp through a colander or sieve, reserving 2-3 tablespoons of oil.
6. Prepare the Hot Sauce according to the recipe.
7. Return the reserved oil to the wok.
8. Add garlic and onion, allowing them to brown slightly.
9. Add water chestnuts, mushrooms, and shrimp back into the wok.
10. Blend in the Hot Sauce and gently flip the contents of the wok for 1-2 minutes or until everything is hot.
11. Place the dish on a serving platter and sprinkle with chopped scallions.
Note: Flipping the wok is preferable to stir-frying, as the food will not be touched during cooking.
